---
title: "VedicDateTime"
subtitle: "An R package to implement the Vedic (lunisolar) calendar system"
---

::: {.hero}

# VedicDateTime

Convert **Gregorian dates** (or **Julian day numbers**) into key **Vedic calendar entities** (Panchanga) in R.

**VedicDateTime** is an open-source framework that implements the Vedic calendar and provides conversions for Gregorian dates, motivated by analyses where both solar and lunar cycles matter (e.g., time-series seasonality and holiday effects).

[Get started](getting-started.qmd){.btn .btn-primary}  
[Reference](reference.qmd){.btn .btn-secondary}

:::

## Highlights

- Implements core Panchanga components and related entities:
  - **Tithi** (lunar day), **Vaara** (weekday), **Nakshatra** (constellation), **Yoga**, **Karana** (half-tithi)
  - Additional entities and helpers used in Vedic calendar computations
- Converts from **Gregorian date-time** or **Julian day number** to Vedic entities
- Designed to support research workflows, including **time-series analysis**

## Quick example


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
# install.packages("VedicDateTime")  # if/when on CRAN
# or install from GitHub (see Getting started)

library(VedicDateTime)

# Typical workflow:
# 1) choose date/time, 2) choose location (latitude, longitude) and timezone
# 3) compute Panchanga entities

# NOTE: Replace with your actual inputs.
# jd <- gregorian_to_jd("2023-01-01 00:00:00", tz = "UTC")
# place <- list(latitude = 12.9716, longitude = 77.5946, timezone = 5.5)
# tithi(jd, place)
```
:::


## Citation

If you use VedicDateTime in academic work, please cite the package paper:

> Bokde, N. D., Patil, P. K., Sengupta, S., Sawant, M., & Feijóo-Lorenzo, A. E. (2023). *VedicDateTime: An R package to implement Vedic calendar system*. Multimedia Tools and Applications. https://doi.org/10.1007/s11042-023-16553-w
